name: Valdelinares — circular desde Mora de Rubielos por la Cascada de la Hiedra
region_intro: Mountain biking around Mora De Rubielos offers diverse terrain within the province of Teruel, Spain, characterized by its striking natural landscapes. The region features dramatic gorges carved by the Rivers Mijares and Rubielos, alongside dense forests of pines and holm oaks. Mountainous terrain with significant elevation changes provides challenging ascents, while charming waterfalls and natural springs offer scenic resting points. This varied landscape ensures a wide array of mountain bike trails Mora de Rubielos popular routes for all skill levels. Best mountain bike trails around Mora De Rubielos The most popular mountain bike trail is Fuen Narices - Cascada de la Hiedra - Balsa Nueva — circular desde Mora de Rubielos por La Virgen de la Vega, a 24.6 miles (39.6 km) trail that takes 3 hours 39 minutes to complete. This moderate route passes by waterfalls and reservoirs, offering varied scenery. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Valdelinares — circular desde Mora de Rubielos por la Cascada de la Hiedra, a difficult 31.9 miles (51.4 km) path. This route features substantial elevation gain through mountainous terrain, suitable for experienced riders. Local mountain bikers also love the Mora de Rubielos Castle – Mora de Rubielos loop from Mora de Rubielos, a 25.6 miles (41.2 km) trail leading through varied landscapes including views of the historic castle, often completed in about 3 hours 52 minutes. Mountain biking around Mora De Rubielos is defined by dramatic gorges, dense forests, and mountainous terrain with significant elevation changes. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy family-friendly routes to challenging ascents. The routes in Mora De Rubielos are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from more than 20 reviews. More than 400 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Mora De Rubielos's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Mora de Rubielos?
A: There are over 15 mountain bike trails recorded in the Mora de Rubielos area on komoot, catering to various skill levels. These routes traverse the diverse landscapes of the Teruel province.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Mora de Rubielos?
A: The terrain around Mora de Rubielos is highly varied. You'll encounter dramatic gorges carved by the Rivers Mijares and Rubielos, dense forests of pines and holm oaks, and mountainous areas with significant elevation changes. Many trails follow well-maintained forest tracks and some secondary roads, offering both challenging ascents and scenic, flowy sections.

Q: Are there different difficulty levels for mountain bike trails in Mora de Rubielos?
A: Yes, the region offers a wide range of difficulty levels. You can find easy routes suitable for beginners, moderate trails that provide a good workout, and difficult paths requiring very good fitness and advanced riding skills. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e996610879/valdelinares-circular-desde-mora-de-rubielos-por-la-cascada-de-la-hiedra" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valdelinares — circular desde Mora de Rubielos por la Cascada de la Hiedra&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging route with substantial elevation gain.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Mora de Rubielos?
A: The best seasons for mountain biking in Mora de Rubielos are generally spring and autumn, when temperatures are milder and the natural scenery is at its most vibrant. Summers can be hot, especially at midday, while winters can bring colder temperatures and potential snow at higher elevations, particularly around peaks like Javalambre.

Q: Are there any family-friendly mountain bike trails in Mora de Rubielos?
A: Yes, there are several easy routes suitable for families. These trails often feature less challenging terrain and shorter distances. An example of an easy route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38180877" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Móra Mountain Bike Workshop loop from Mora de Rubielos&lt;/a&gt;, which offers a pleasant ride without excessive difficulty.

Q: Can I find circular mountain bike routes in Mora de Rubielos?
A: Absolutely. Many of the mountain bike trails in Mora de Rubielos are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. A popular moderate circular route is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1005045459/fuen-narices-cascada-de-la-hiedra-balsa-nueva-circular-desde-mora-de-rubielos-por-la-virgen-de-la-vega" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuen Narices - Cascada de la Hiedra - Balsa Nueva&lt;/a&gt;, which offers varied scenery including waterfalls and reservoirs.

Q: Are there mountain bike routes that pass by waterfalls or natural springs?
A: Yes, the region is known for its charming waterfalls and natural springs. Routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1005045459/fuen-narices-cascada-de-la-hiedra-balsa-nueva-circular-desde-mora-de-rubielos-por-la-virgen-de-la-vega" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuen Narices - Cascada de la Hiedra - Balsa Nueva&lt;/a&gt; specifically pass by the Cascada de la Hiedra. You might also encounter natural springs like Fuente de la Salud (&lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4374209" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuente de la Salud&lt;/a&gt;) along various trails, offering pleasant spots for rest.

Q: What natural features or scenic points can I see along the trails?
A: Mora de Rubielos offers spectacular natural beauty. You can expect to see dramatic gorges, dense pine and holm oak forests, and panoramic views from mountainous peaks. Highlights include the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4374269" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Gorge&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5805644" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñarroya Summit and Observation Tower&lt;/a&gt;, which provide breathtaking vistas.

Q: Are there any historical landmarks or castles accessible by mountain bike?
A: Yes, the region is rich in history. The impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5113901" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mora de Rubielos Castle&lt;/a&gt; is a prominent landmark and can be incorporated into cycling routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14710186" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mora de Rubielos Castle – Mora de Rubielos loop&lt;/a&gt;. You might also pass through charming medieval villages.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy the most about mountain biking in Mora de Rubielos?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 20 reviews. Reviewers often praise the diverse terrain, the stunning natural landscapes including gorges and forests, and the variety of routes available for all skill levels. The presence of historical landmarks like the Mora de Rubielos Castle also adds to the appeal.

Q: Is there dedicated parking for mountain bike trails in Mora de Rubielos?
A: While specific dedicated parking for every trailhead isn't always marked, Mora de Rubielos and its surrounding villages typically offer public parking areas where you can leave your vehicle before starting your ride. It's advisable to check local signage upon arrival.

Q: Are there services for cyclists in the Mora de Rubielos area?
A: Yes, the region is increasingly catering to cyclists. Some outdoor activity providers in the surrounding area offer services such as bike storage, workshops for repairs, and washing facilities, making it convenient for mountain bikers visiting the area.
tips: The beautiful Turolense town of Alcalà de la Selva is located in the Gúdar-Javalambre region and is under the watchful eye of the Heredia castle of Muslim and medieval origin. We can also visit the Hermitages of Nuestra Señora de Loreto and San Roque or the Church of San Simón and San Judas.

The Valdelinares ski resort is closed during the ski season, but they leave the parking lots open for those adventurers who dare to discover these places, embark on a hiking route or go for a bike ride.
Built in 1985, it is currently used for forest service in the event of fires in the surrounding forests. A good stop to cool off in the summer months.
